detail: A6 requires intersecting lines at a single point with no triangle/parallel invocation. Clone is a right-triangle altitude / geometric-mean problem, which belongs to A5.T3 (right-triangle altitude similarity), not A6.
Clone is a triangle-area inequality (area = (1/2)·6·h), not an intersecting-lines / vertical-angle problem. Does not test A6 (intersecting lines at a point).

Hard A3.T1 variant requires overlapping/intersecting triangles with two combined isosceles arguments, or a nested config requiring exterior-angle plus angle-sum chaining. Clone is a single isosceles triangle with one exterior-angle step — matches medium/easy difficulty at best.

Violates must_preserve 'explicit parallel statement' and 'single transversal' for A1.T1. Stem only describes two angles forming a straight line — pure linear-pair problem with no parallel lines or transversal. Belongs in A6.

A3 requires isosceles/equilateral side-equality. Clone uses triangle similarity (angle transfer via similar triangles) with no isosceles side-equality — belongs to A5.T1 (angle transfer via similarity).

Violates must_preserve 'parallel-lines configuration'. Clone has only a linear pair (no parallel lines, no transversal). Also fails to require solving for a parameter — the linear-pair sum is given directly. Belongs in A6.
